title: selaginella leucoloma
intro: Selaginella leucoloma, also known as Resurrection Fern, is a perennial, rhizomatous plant belonging to the Selaginellaceae family, native to tropical and subtropical regions of Africa and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, which have been attributed to its high concentration of saponins and flavonoids. Consuming the plant has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and improving digestion, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. The herbal preparations of Selaginella leucoloma include tea infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential interactions with other medications.
